Element.name: corpus_name
Element.Documentation: Description: Full name of the corpus; Further details and examples: International Corpus of Learner English, with multiple entries for different names in different languages

Element.name: corpus_acronym
Element.Documentation: Description: Corpus acronym; Further details and examples: ICLE

Element.name: corpus_version
Element.Documentation: Description: Corpus version; Further details and examples: One possible approach to versioning is to use digits such that the first digit (e.g. 1.0) represents a major corpus update (adding new data, new annotations, etc.) and the second digit represents a minor update (corrections of annotations). If relevant, a third digit can be added for changes in documentation etc. (but only in cases where data has not been changed).

Element.name: corpus_pid
Element.Documentation: Description: Persistent identifier; Further details and examples: A persistent identifier is typically a DOI or a handle, e.g. http://hdl.handle.net/20.500.12124/25

Element.name: corpus_cite_as
Element.Documentation: Description: Corpus citation; Further details and examples: How to cite this corpus: Author(s) or Responsible(s) (Year). Title of the data set (Version number): Distributor, Identifier or Location. This is typically generated by the data repository.

Element.name: corpus_ref_article
Element.Documentation: Description: Reference article; Further details and examples: Reference articles typically contain an extensive verbal description of the corpus and its design.

Element.name: corpus_description
Element.Documentation: Description: Short description of the corpus

Element.name: corpus_documentation
Element.Documentation: Description: Link to more extensive corpus documentation; Further details and examples: https://www.porta.eurac.edu/lci/leonide/

Element.name: corpus_author
Element.Documentation: Description: Corpus creator; Further details and examples: Max Mustermann

Element.name: corpus_contributor
Element.Documentation: Description: Corpus contributor; Further details and examples: (Jane Doe, transcription of english texts)

Element.name: corpus_contact_mail
Element.Documentation: Description: Contact e-mail address; Further details and examples: porta@eurac.edu

Element.name: corpus_publisher
Element.Documentation: Description: Person or entity responsible for the corpus; Further details and examples: Institute for Applied Linguistics, Eurac Research

Element.name: corpus_availability
Element.Documentation: Description: Licensing terms and conditions; Fixed attributes: open, restricted, closed

Element.name: corpus_access_requirements
Element.Documentation: Description: Access requirements; Condition: if corpus_availability = "restricted"; Further details and examples: academic use

Element.name: corpus_licence
Element.Documentation: Description: Licence name; Further details and examples: ACA-BY-NC-NORED

Element.name: corpus_licence_url
Element.Documentation: Description: Link to licence text; Further details and examples: http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/

Element.name: corpus_date_of_publication
Element.Documentation: Description: Date of publication; Further details and examples: e.g. YYYY, YYYY-MM or YYYY-MM-DD

Element.name: corpus_other_versions
Element.Documentation: Description: Associated version; Further details and examples: (1.0, link to version)

Element.name: corpus_character_encoding
Element.Documentation: Descripyion: Character encoding; Further details and examples: utf-8, latin, ascii, ...

Component.name: corpus_related_research_project
Component.Documentation: Description: Research project related to the corpus

Element.name: corpus_related_research_project_name
Element.Documentation: Description: Name of related research project; Further details and examples: MERLIN

Element.name: corpus_related_research_project_URL
Element.Documentation: Description: Link to related research project; Condition: if corpus_related_research_project_name = ""; Further details and examples: https://merlin-platform.eu/

Element.name: corpus_file_format
Element.Documentation: Description: File format; Further details and examples: txt, csv, tiff, xml, JSON...

Element.name: corpus_file_format_specifications
Element.Documentation: Description: File format specification; Further details and examples: TEI P5, the BAWE corpus manual (https://www.reading.ac.uk/internal/appling/bawe/BAWE.documentation.pdf), the SWELL corpus (SVALA JSON format, https://ep.liu.se/ecp/159/023/ecp18159023.pdf)

Element.name: corpus_target_language
Element.Documentation: Description: Target language; Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3], other; Further details and examples: en; use “other” for under-represented Ls

Element.name: corpus_L1_language
Element.Documentation: Description: L1 language; Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3], other; Further details and examples: en; use “other” for under-represented Ls

Element.name: corpus_size
Element.Documentation: Description: Corpus size; Further details and examples: Storage requirement in KB, MB, GB, TB or PB

Element.name: corpus_size_tokens
Element.Documentation: Description: Number of tokens; Further details and examples: Only count learner language but not prompts, interviewers, etc.

Element.name: corpus_size_texts
Element.Documentation: Description: Number of learner productions; Further details and examples: 9529

Element.name: corpus_size_learners
Element.Documentation: Description: Number of learners; Further details and examples: 163

Element.name: corpus_mode
Element.Documentation: Description: Corpus modality; Fixed attributes: written, spoken, signed, multimodal

Element.name: corpus_transcription
Element.Documentation: Description: Were the language samples included in the corpus transcribed?; Fixed attributes: true, false

Element.name: corpus_transcription_guidelines
Element.Documentation: Description: Link to transcription guidelines; Condition: if corpus_transcription = true; Fixed attributes: readme file, corpus documentation, NA

Element.name: corpus_privacy_protection
Element.Documentation: Documentation: Were the texts (pseudo-)anonymized?; Fixed attributes: true, false

Element.name: corpus_privacy_protection_documentation
Element.Documentation: Description: Link to privacy protection documentation; if corpus_privacy_protection = true; Further details and examples: Examples include guidelines, consent form, etc.

Element.name: corpus_single_or_multi_author
Element.Documentation: Description: Were individual texts written by a single author?; Condition: if corpus_mode = "written"; Fixed attributes: single author, multiple author

Element.name: corpus_text_versioning
Element.Documentation: Description: Does the corpus include several versions of the same text by the same learner?; Condition: if corpus_mode = "written"; Fixed attributes: true, false; Further details and examples: Please also answer true to this question if the corpus includes texts with revisions identified by clear mark-up.

Element.name: corpus_longitudinal
Element.Documentation: Description: Does the corpus contain longitudinal data?; Fixed attributes: true, false; ; Further details and examples: Please also answer true to this question if the corpus includes texts with revisions identified by clear mark-up.

Element.name: corpus_time_of_data_collection
Element.Documentation: Description: Time period of data collection; Further details and examples: 2015, 2011-2012, November 2013, 20-10-2020

Element.name: corpus_place_of_data_collection
Element.Documentation: Description: Place of data collection; Further details and examples: Place of data collection could indicate the city or country of data collection depending on how relevant this data is for understanding the corpus data and design

Element.name: corpus_specialized_language_use
Element.Documentation: Description: Domain of language use; Further details and examples: Domains of use will typically correspond to academic disciplines.

Element.name: corpus_production_setting
Element.Documentation: Description: Language production setting; Fixed attributes: language instruction, other educational setting, official language testing, naturalistic setting; Further details and examples: Official language testing refers to a situation where language testing is performed with an approved language assessment body.

Element.name: corpus_language_testing_setting
Element.Documentation: Description: Full name of the official language test; Condition: if corpus_production_setting = "official language testing"; Further details and examples: Test of English as a Foreign Language/Cambridge Advanced Certificate, International English Language Testing System, Certificato di Conoscenza della Lingua Italiana

Element.name: corpus_proficiency_level
Element.Documentation: Description: Coarse proficiency levels; Fixed attributes: beginner, intermediate, advanced, unknown; Further details and examples: Provide an approximate level of proficiency even if proficiency scores are not available for the individual learners/texts. This variable is meant as a general indicator.

Element.name: corpus_proficiency_assignment_available
Element.Documentation: Description: Does the corpus contain proficiency scores for individual learners or texts?; Fixed attributes: true, false

Element.name: corpus_proficiency_assignment_method
Element.Documentation: Description: Method of proficiency level assignment; Condition: if corpus_proficiency_assignment_available = true; Fixed attributes: learner-centred method, text-centred method; Further details and examples: Learner-centred methods relate to methods by which a proficiency level is assigned to each learner independently of the collected text. Text-centred methods refer to methods by which proficiency scores are assigned to individual texts (cf. Carlsen 2012).

Element.name: corpus_learner_proficiency_assignment_method
Element.Documentation: Description: How was the proficiency of the learners evaluated?; Condition: if corpus_proficiency_assignment_method="learner-centred method"; Fixed attributes: independent instrument, total test score, other; Further details and examples: Select "total test score" for contexts where the text was written for an official language test with other components

Element.name: corpus_learner_proficiency_assignment_instrument
Element.Documentation: Description: Name of instrument used to evaluate learners’ proficiency; Condition: if corpus_learner_proficiency_assignment_method = "independent instrument" OR " total test score"; Further details and examples: Oxford Quick Placement Test, DIALANG, Vocabulary Level Test, Cambridge English, TOEFL, IELTS, in-house…

Element.name: corpus_learner_proficiency_documentation
Element.Documentation: Description: Link to relevant documentation on how learners were evaluated; Condition: if corpus_proficiency_assignment_method = "learner-centred method"

Element.name: corpus_text_proficiency_assignment_method
Element.Documentation: Description: How were the texts evaluated? Condition: if corpus_proficiency_assignment_method = "text-centred method"; Fixed attributes: score on text, teacher's evaluation, post-hoc assignment; Further details and examples: Select "total test score" for contexts where the text was written for an official language test with other components

Element.name: corpus_text_proficiency_assignment_instrument
Element.Documentation: Description: Name of instrument used to evaluate texts’ proficiency; Condition: if corpus_proficiency_assignment_method = "text-centred method"; Further details and examples: Cambridge English, TOEFL,…

Element.name: corpus_text_proficiency_documentation
Element.Documentation: Description: Link to relevant documentation on how texts were evaluated (descriptors, rubric, etc.); Condition: if corpus_proficiency_assignment_method = "text-centred method"; Further details and examples: https://merlin-platform.eu/docs/MERLIN_Rating-Grid_DE.pdf

Element.name: corpus_motivation_test
Element.Documentation: Description: Reference to motivation test conducted

Element.name: corpus_attitude_test
Element.Documentation: Description: Reference to test of attitude conducted (including affective differences)

Element.name: corpus_aptitude_test
Element.Documentation: Description: Reference to aptitude test conducted (including other types of cognitive differences)

Element.name: corpus_comparable_data_included
Element.Documentation: Description: Does the corpus include comparison data?; Fixed attributes: true, false; Further details and examples: Comparison data typically refers to productions by native speakers or expert writers, etc.

Element.name: corpus_related
Element.Documentation: Description: Link to other comparable corpora; Further details and examples: Comparable corpora can be of various forms, including same tasks performed by a different sample of writers/speakers, same tasks in other languages, data from related projects, etc.

Element.name: text_id
Element.Documentation: Description: Unique identifier for the text

Element.name: text_file
Element.Documentation: Description: Associated files (copies of handwritten texts in pdf, transcriptions, sound files, videos, etc.)

Element.name: learner_id
Element.Documentation: Description: Reference to unique (anonymous) identifier for the author of the text

Element.name: transcriber_id
Element.Documentation: Description: Reference to unique identifier for the transcriber; Condition: if corpus_transcription = true

Element.name: situation_id
Element.Documentation: Description: Reference to unique identifier for situational and task characteristics

Element.name: annotation_id
Element.Documentation: Description: Reference to unique identifier for annotations provided for the text; Further details and examples: TH1, TH2, lemma, pos

Element.name: annotator_id
Element.Documentation: Description: Reference to unique identifier for the manual annotator of this document

Element.name: text_language
Element.Documentation: Description: Language; Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3]

Element.name: text_version
Element.Documentation: Description: Text version number; Condition: if corpus_text_versioning = true

Element.name: text_time_of_creation
Element.Documentation: Description: Time of creation

Element.name: text_place_of_data_collection
Element.Documentation: Description: Place of data collection; Further details and examples: Place of data collection could indicate the city or country of data collection

Element.name: text_tool
Element.Documentation: Description: Tool(s) used to produce the text; Fixed attributes: monolingual dictionary, bilingual dictionary, grammar, spell- or grammar-checker, corpus/corpus interface, language forum, automatic translation, AI tool

Element.name: text_proficiency
Element.Documentation: Description: Proficiency score or level for the text; Condition: if proficiency_assignment_method = "text-centred"

Element.name: text_official_language_testing_score
Element.Documentation: Description: Separate test score for the text, if written as part of an official language test; Condition: if data_production_setting = "language testing setting"; Further details and examples: Levels for CEFR, figures for IELTS, etc.

Element.name: text_proficiency_CEFR_conversion
Element.Documentation: Description: Conversion of text proficiency score to CEFR; Fixed attributes: A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2

Element.name: text_note
Element.Documentation: Description: Any comments on the text and its origins

Element.name: text_token_count
Element.Documentation: Description: Number of tokens in the text

Element.name: learner_id
Element.Documentation: Description: Unique identifier

Element.name: learner_age
Element.Documentation: Description: Age at text production

Element.name: learner_gender
Element.Documentation: Description: Gender; Fixed attributes: female, male, non-binary

Element.name: learner_educational_background
Element.Documentation: Description: Highest level of education; Fixed attributes: no schooling, early childhood education, primary education, lower secondary education, upper secondary education, post-secondary non-tertiary education, short-cycle tertiary education, Bachelor's degree or equivalent level, Master's degree or equivalent level, Doctoral degree or equivalent level, not elsewhere classified

Element.name: learner_socioeconomic_status
Element.Documentation: Description:Socio-economic status; Further details and examples: Description of the learner’s perceived socio-economic status. For example, the CHAT format uses working class, middle class, upper class, and limited income (MacWhinney, 2000).

Element.name: learner_country
Element.Documentation: Description: Country; Fixed attributes: ISO 3166 country codes

Element.name: learner_region
Element.Documentation: Description: Region; Further details and examples: Geographical details about the learner if relevant to their language use

Element.name: learner_environment
Element.Documentation: Description: Learner environment; Further details and examples: Is the learner living in an urban or rural environment?

Element.name: learner_impairment
Element.Documentation: Description: Does the learner have an impairment?; Fixed attributes: true, false

Element.name: learner_impairment_details
Element.Documentation: Description: Learner impairment; Condition: if learner_impairment = true; Further details and examples: Preferably choose among our list of examples. You can use tuples to provide more info about the conditions (e.g. {hearing impairment, permanent}). Examples: hearing impairment/speech impairment/vision impairment/other impairments

Element.name: learner_atypical_learning
Element.Documentation: Description: Is the learner characterized by atypical learning or atypical (cognitive) development?; Fixed attributes: true, false

Element.name: learner_atypical_learning_details
Element.Documentation: Description: Type of atypical learning or atypical development; Condition: if learner_atypical_learning = true; Further details and examples: Examples: speech sound disorder, language disorder, dyslexia, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder

Element.name: learner_target_language
Element.Documentation: Description: Learner target language; Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3]

Element.name: learner_target_language_instructed
Element.Documentation: Description: Was the learner in an instructed learning context at the time of data collection?; Fixed attributes: true, false

Element.name: learner_target_language_immersive_instruction
Element.Documentation: Description: Was the learner in an immersive instructed learning context at the time of data collection?; Condition: if learner_target_language_instructed = true; Fixed attributes: true, false; Further details and examples: Language immersion is a method used to teach students a second language where the language being taught is primarily used during teaching.

Element.name: learner_target_language_environment
Element.Documentation: Description: Was the learner in a country/region where the target language is spoken at the time of data collection?; Fixed attributes: true, false

Element.name: months_spent_target_language_environment
Element.Documentation: Description: Cumulated time spent in an environment where the target language is spoken in months

Element.name: learner_target_language_onset
Element.Documentation: Description: Age of onset of acquisition

Element.name: learner_target_language_learning_context
Element.Documentation: Description: Learning context of target language from onset to current age; Further details and examples: Use tuples and choose among instructed, immersive and naturalistic; e.g. {12- 20: instructed}

Element.name: learner_target_language_use_context
Element.Documentation: Context of use of the target language from onset to current age; Fixed attributes: family, friends, leisure, public, work, education; Further details and examples: List all contexts from onset to current age (use tuples if you want to add extra info related to time spent on these different contexts of use); e.g. {12-20: education, leisure}

Element.name: learner_target_language_listening_experience
Element.Documentation: Description: Listening experience; Further details and examples: Recommended scale: Never, rarely, once a month, once a week, daily

Element.name: learner_target_language_speaking_experience
Element.Documentation: Description: Speaking experience; Further details and examples: Recommended scale: Never, rarely, once a month, once a week, daily

Element.name: learner_target_language_reading_experience
Element.Documentation: Description: Reading experience; Further details and examples: Recommended scale: Never, rarely, once a month, once a week, daily

Element.name: learner_target_language_writing_experience
Element.Documentation: Description: Writing experience; Further details and examples: Recommended scale: Never, rarely, once a month, once a week, daily

Element.name: learner_target_language_use_social_media
Element.Documentation: Description: Social media experience; Further details and examples: Recommended scale: Never, rarely, once a month, once a week, daily

Element.name: learner_target_language_proficiency
Element.Documentation: Description: Learner proficiency; Condition: if corpus_proficiency_assignment_method = "learner-centred method"; Further details and examples: Results of language test, in-house test, or self-assessment

Element.name: learner_target_language_proficiency_CEFR_conversion
Element.Documentation: Description: Learner proficiency (CEFR); Condition: if corpus_proficiency_assignment_method = "learner-centred method"; Fixed attributes: A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2; Further details and examples: If possible, provide proficiency converted to CEFR

Element.name: learner_target_language_official_language_testing_score
Element.Documentation: Description: Official language test score; Condition: if corpus_production_setting = "official language testing"

Element.name: learner_L1
Element.Documentation: Description: Learner L1; Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3]; Further details and examples: L1 often refers to the language(s) that a person learns from birth or infancy, which is/are also often their primary and most proficient language(s). However, there is some variability in the definition of the term in L2 research. Please use the corpus documentation to provide more details about your understanding of this term if relevant.

Element.name: learner_parent_L1
Element.Documentation: Description: Parent L1; Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3]; Further details and examples: Use tuple of (parent, language ISO code) if you want to add more info, e.g. [mother, eng], [father, spa]; [mother, eng], [mother2, ger]

Element.name: learner_home_language
Element.Documentation: Description: Language spoken at home; Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3]; Further details and examples: Use tuple of (language ISO code, percentage) if you want to add more info

Element.name: learner_language_friends
Element.Documentation: Description: Language used with friends; Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3]; Further details and examples: Use tuple of (language ISO code, percentage) if you want to add more info

Element.name: learner_language_education_kindergarten
Element.Documentation: Description: Language of education (kindergarten); Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3]; Further details and examples: Use tuple of (language ISO code, percentage) if you want to add more info

Element.name: learner_language_education_primary
Element.Documentation: Description: Language of education (primary school); Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3]; Further details and examples: Use tuple of (language ISO code, percentage) if you want to add more info

Element.name: learner_language_education_secondary
Element.Documentation: Description: Language of education (secondary school); Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3]; Further details and examples: Use tuple of (language ISO code, percentage) if you want to add more info

Element.name: learner_language_education_higher
Element.Documentation: Description: Language of education (higher education); Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3]; Further details and examples: Use tuple of (language ISO code, percentage) if you want to add more info

Component.name: learner_L2
Component.Documentation: L2s that are not the target language

Element.name: learner_L2
Element.Documentation: Description: L2 that is not the target language; Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3]

Element.name: learner_L2_language_onset
Element.Documentation: Description: Age of onset of acquisition

Element.name: learner_L2_language_learning_context
Element.Documentation: Description: Learning context of target language from onset to current age;; Further details and examples: Use tuples and choose among instructed, immersive and naturalistic, e.g. {12- 20: instructed}

Element.name: learner_L2_language_use_context
Element.Documentation: Description: Context of use of the target language from onset to current age; Fixed attributes: family,friends, leisure, public, work, education; Further details and examples: List all contexts from onset to current age (use tuples if you want to add extra info related to time spent on these different contexts of use), e.g. {12-20: education, leisure}

Element.name: learner_L2_language_listening_experience
Element.Documentation: Description: Listening experience; Further details and examples: Recommended scale: Never, rarely, once a month, once a week, daily

Element.name: learner_L2_language_speaking_experience
Element.Documentation: Description: Speaking experience; Further details and examples: Recommended scale: Never, rarely, once a month, once a week, daily

Element.name: learner_L2_language_reading_experience
Element.Documentation: Description: Reading experience; Further details and examples: Recommended scale: Never, rarely, once a month, once a week, daily

Element.name: learner_L2_language_writing_experience
Element.Documentation: Description: Writing experience; Further details and examples: Recommended scale: Never, rarely, once a month, once a week, daily

Element.name: learner_L2_CMC
Element.Documentation: Description: Social media experience; Further details and examples: Recommended scale: Never, rarely, once a month, once a week, daily

Element.name: learner_L2_language_proficiency
Element.Documentation: Description: Learner proficiency; Further details and examples: Results of language test, in-house test, or self-assessment

Element.name: learner_L2_language_proficiency_CEFR_conversion
Element.Documentation: Description: Learner proficiency (CEFR); Fixed attributes: A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2; Further details and examples: If possible, provide proficiency converted to CEFR

Element.name: learner_L2_months_spent_L2_environment
Element.Documentation: Description: Cumulated time spent in an environment where L2 is spoken in months

Element.name: learner_motivation
Element.Documentation: Description: Motivation test score; Condition: if corpus_motivation_test defined; Further details and examples: Use tuples if necessary

Element.name: learner_attitude
Element.Documentation: Description: Attitude test score; Condition: if corpus_attitude_test defined; Further details and examples: Use tuples if necessary

Element.name: learner_aptitude
Element.Documentation: Description: Aptitude test score; Condition: if corpus_aptitude_test defined; Further details and examples: Use tuples if necessary

Element.name: learner_note
Element.Documentation: Description: Any comments regarding this learner; Further details and examples: This could include a learner biography, more details about their mutlingual profile, etc.

Element.name: situation_id
Element.Documentation: Description: Unique identifier for the language situation

Element.name: situation_participants_addressor
Element.Documentation: Description: Number of addressors; Fixed attributes: single, two, more than two; Further details and examples: Authorial persona, author

Element.name: situation_participants_addressee
Element.Documentation: Description: Number of addressees; Fixed attributes: single, plural, un-enumerated

Element.name: situation_participants_addressee_type
Element.Documentation: Description: Personal relationship with addressees; Condition: if situation_participants_addressee_type = "plural" or "un-enumerated"; Fixed attributes: classmates, student-teacher, colleagues, family members, friends, strangers

Element.name: situation_participants_onlookers
Element.Documentation: Description: Are there on-lookers?; Fixed attributes: true, false

Element.name: situation_mode
Element.Documentation: Description: Mode, Fixed attributes: speech, writing

Element.name: situation_medium
Element.Documentation: Description: Medium of communication; Fixed attributes: typed, handwritten, e-mail, text messaging (phone service), instant messaging (online), face-to-face, video conferencing, blogging, discussion forum, telephone; Further details and examples: Select as many as relevant

Element.name: situation_planning_type
Element.Documentation: Description: Situation circumstances; Fixed attributes: real time, planned, scripted, revised and edited

Element.name: situation_setting
Element.Documentation: Description: Situation settings; Fixed attributes: personal, public, occupational, educational; Further details and examples: Personal settings include family, social networks, friends and leisure. Public spaces include streets, public transports, shops, cinemas, restaurants, etc. Occupational settings refer to the work environment. Educational settings refer to any school or institution where education is provided.

Element.name: situation_purpose
Element.Documentation: Description: Communicative purpose; Fixed attributes: narrate/report, describe, inform/explain/interpret, persuade, how-to/procedural, entertain, edify, reveal self, seek information/enquire, negotiate/reach an agreement, other

Element.name: situation_purpose_specific
Element.Documentation: Description: Specific purpose; Further details and examples: describe methods, go shopping, tell a story, solve a problem, order food in a restaurant

Element.name: situation_topic_domain
Element.Documentation: Description: General topical domain; Fixed attributes: domestic, daily activities, business/work place, science, education / academic, government / legal / politics, religion, sports, art / entertainement, other

Element.name: situation_topic_specific
Element.Documentation: Description: Specific topic

Element.name: situation_register
Element.Documentation: Description: Register; Fixed attributes: essay, summary, translation, creative writing, scientific writing, conversation, monologue, other

Element.name: situation_register_specific
Element.Documentation: Description: (Sub-)register or task type; Further details and examples: letter of application, MA dissertation, interview, argumentative essay, text messaging, ...

Element.name: task_instructions
Element.Documentation: Description: Task instructions; Further details and examples: Please provide detailed information about the task (e.g. prompt, description)

Element.name: task_material
Element.Documentation: Description: Link to task materials; Further details and examples: Link or references to any supporting documents provided as a prompt for the task (picture used for picture story/description, text used to write a summary, etc.)

Element.name: task_duration
Element.Documentation: Description: Was the task timed or not? Fixed attributes: true, false

Element.name: task_duration_minutes
Element.Documentation: Description: Task duration in minutes; Condition: if task_duration = true

Element.name: task_ref_tools_allowed
Element.Documentation: Description: Was the learner allowed to use reference tools?; Fixed attributes: true, false

Element.name: task_ref_tools
Element.Documentation: Description: List of reference tools the learner was allowed to use; Condition: if task_ref_tools = true; Further details and examples: monolingual dictionary, bilingual dictionary, grammar, spell- or grammar-checker, corpus/corpus interface, language forum, automatic translation, AI tool

Element.name: task_assessed
Element.Documentation: Description: Was the task part of an exam or any other type of language assessment settings?; Fixed attributes: true, false

Element.name: task_note
Element.Documentation: any comments regarding this situation/task

Element.name: annotation_type
Element.Documentation: Description: Unique identifier for annotation; Fixed attributes: Lemma, POS, UPOS, Parsing, Error, TH, other

Element.name: annotation_automatic
Element.Documentation: Description: Was the annotation process fully automatic? Fixed attributes: true, false

Element.name: annotation_tool
Element.Documentation: Description: Name of tool used for annotation; Further details and examples: TreeTagger, WordSmith Tools, Spacy

Element.name: annotation_tool_version
Element.Documentation: Description: Version of tool used for annotation; Further details and examples: tree-tagger-MacOSX-Intel-3.2.3

Element.name: annotator_id
Element.Documentation: Description: Unique identifier for manual annotator

Element.name: annotation_evaluation
Element.Documentation: Description: Was the annotation evaluated?; Fixed attributes: true, false

Element.name: annotation_corrected
Element.Documentation: Description: Was the automatic annotation subject to further correction?; Fixed attributes: true, false

Element.name: annotation_documentation
Element.Documentation: Description: Plain text description of annotation or link to documentation; Further details and examples: In case you answer “yes” to the previous questions, it is particularly important that you document the evaluation of the annotation as well as its correction.

Element.name: annotator_id
Element.Documentation: Description: Unique identifier for annotator

Element.name: annotator_L1
Element.Documentation: Description: Annotator L1; Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3]

Element.name: annotator_target_language_competence
Element.Documentation: Description: Annotator target language competence; Fixed attributes: A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2

Element.name: annotator_type
Element.Documentation: Description: Annotator experience; Further details and examples: e.g. expert, trained, student, teacher, crowdsourcing…

Element.name: annotator_L2
Element.Documentation: Description: Annotator L2; Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3]

Element.name: annotator_note
Element.Documentation: Description: Any comments regarding the annotator; Further details and examples: This could include more details about their mutlingual profile

Element.name: transcriber_id
Element.Documentation: Description: Unique identifier for transcriber

Element.name: transcriber_L1
Element.Documentation: Description: Transcriber L1; Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3]

Element.name: transcriber_target_language_competence
Element.Documentation: Description: Transcriber target language competence; Fixed attributes: A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2

Element.name: transcriber_type
Element.Documentation: Description: Transcriber experience; Further details and examples: e.g. expert, trained, student, teacher, crowdsourcing…

Element.name: transcriber_L2
Element.Documentation: Description: Transcriber L2; Fixed attributes: [ISO language code 639-3]

Element.name: transcriber_note
Element.Documentation: Description: Any comments regarding the transcriber; Further details and examples: This could include more details about their mutlingual profile
